async_execution.mechanism: Windsurf hooks run synchronously; no async/background execution documented

context_injection.mechanism: Windsurf hooks cannot inject context into the agent's active session

decision_control.mechanism: Windsurf hooks are observational; no mechanism to block, allow, or modify the triggering action documented

handler_types.mechanism: Windsurf hooks execute shell commands only; no HTTP, LLM prompt, or agent handler types documented

hook_scopes.mechanism: three_config_scopes: global (user-wide), workspace, and managed/enterprise hook configuration scopes

input_modification.mechanism: Windsurf hooks cannot modify tool input arguments before execution

json_io_protocol.mechanism: json_stdin_context: Windsurf hooks receive event context as JSON on stdin

matcher_patterns.mechanism: Windsurf hooks fire on all events of their configured type; no per-tool or regex matching documented

permission_control.mechanism: Windsurf hooks do not participate in permission decisions

providerExtensions.name: Twelve hook event types
providerExtensions.description: Windsurf Cascade Hooks support twelve named event types covering the full agent action lifecycle: pre_read_code, post_read_code, pre_write_code, post_write_code, pre_run_command, post_run_command, pre_mcp_tool_use, post_mcp_tool_use, pre_user_prompt, post_cascade_response, post_cascade_response_with_transcript, and post_setup_worktree. Pre-hooks (pre_user_prompt, pre_read_code, pre_write_code, pre_run_command, pre_mcp_tool_use) can block the action by exiting with code 2. Post-hooks cannot block since the action has already occurred.

providerExtensions.name: JSON context delivered via stdin
providerExtensions.description: Each hook receives a JSON object on stdin with common fields (agent_action_name, trajectory_id, execution_id, timestamp, tool_info) plus event-specific fields in tool_info. For example, pre_write_code includes file_path and an edits array with old_string/new_string pairs; pre_run_command includes command_line and cwd; pre_mcp_tool_use includes mcp_server_name, mcp_tool_name, and mcp_tool_arguments. Scripts can be written in any language (Python, Bash, Node.js, any executable).

providerExtensions.name: System, user, and workspace config scopes merged in order
providerExtensions.description: Hook configurations are JSON files loaded from three scopes and merged together. System-level (enterprise, managed by IT): macOS /Library/Application Support/Windsurf/hooks.json, Linux/WSL /etc/windsurf/hooks.json, Windows C:\ProgramData\Windsurf\hooks.json. User-level (personal): ~/.codeium/windsurf/hooks.json (Windsurf IDE) or ~/.codeium/hooks.json (JetBrains plugin). Workspace-level (project, version-controlled): .windsurf/hooks.json in the workspace root. Hooks from all levels are combined and execute in order: cloud dashboard → system → user → workspace.

providerExtensions.name: Cloud dashboard distribution for enterprise teams
providerExtensions.description: Enterprise teams can configure Cascade Hooks directly from the Windsurf Team Settings dashboard without managing local files. Requires Enterprise plan and TEAM_SETTINGS_UPDATE permission. Cloud-configured hooks are automatically distributed to all team members and loaded first (before system, user, and workspace hooks). Multiple team configs are merged per action rather than overwritten.

providerExtensions.name: Full conversation transcript hook variant
providerExtensions.description: post_cascade_response_with_transcript is a distinct variant of post_cascade_response that writes the full conversation transcript (from conversation start) to a local JSONL file at ~/.windsurf/transcripts/{trajectory_id}.jsonl and delivers the file path in tool_info rather than inline markdown. The JSONL format includes structured step-level data (file contents, command outputs, tool arguments, applied rules). Files are written with 0600 permissions; the directory is capped at 100 files with oldest pruned first. Designed for enterprise audit and compliance logging.
